title: Use lap-group ranking plus sigmoid-calibrated regime blend
summary: Pit calls are partly relative within a lap and race state, but final submissions are scored by global ROC AUC.
rationale: Pit calls are partly relative within a lap and race state, but final submissions are scored by global ROC AUC. CatBoost ranking objectives let you learn within-group ordering, while scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration preserves ranking whereas isotonic can introduce ties and alter AUC. This supports a two-model blend: local ranker for relative urgency, classifier for global probability shape.
implementation_hint: Train a ranker with groups like Year-Race-LapNumber or Year-Race-LapNumber-CompoundBucket, using PairLogit/QueryAUC-style objectives. Convert rank scores to standardized logits, then blend with the main classifier using out-of-fold sigmoid calibration, optionally with separate calibrators for dry vs wet and neutralized vs green regimes.
expected_effect: Should sharpen ordering among same-lap contenders without sacrificing AUC when merged through rank-preserving calibration.
risk: Some laps have no or many positives, making grouping noisy; over-grouping can hurt if pit calls are not truly competitive within that slice.
